developerEntities: WhizSolve Pte. Ltd. (Singapore)
ByteDance Ltd. (parent)
appStoreId: Android package: app.homework.solve (Google Play, now unpublished). No confirmed original Apple App Store id for the ByteDance version. NOTE: Apple App Store id6473076072 ("SnapSolve: Homework Solver AI", by CamelCake/SnapSolve Labs/Abdellatif Abdelfattah) is an UNRELATED later app, NOT ByteDance.
description: ByteDance's photo-homework app for Indian students in grades 6-12. ByteDance closed it in 2021 when it quit India's edtech market.
killedReason: ByteDance wound down its entire India edtech business. The decision was communicated internally to the India edtech team ("over three dozen" people per reports) on Monday Nov 22, 2021, with most/all staff laid off; the move followed weeks of discussions that had explored migrating the India team to ByteDance's European edtech team. Underlying causes: the June 2020 India ban on TikTok and other ByteDance apps choked distribution and made the environment for a Chinese-owned product in India untenable, and the app failed to gain traction in a crowded doubt-solving market. The shutdown left Resso as the only remaining ByteDance consumer product in India at the time. Consistent with ByteDance's broader 2021 global edtech retreat following China's July 2021 "double reduction" (双减) tutoring crackdown.
notes:
VERIFIED from scratch. HIGH confidence this was a genuine ByteDance product and that it was shut down in India in Nov 2021. ByteDance ownership corroborated by TechCrunch (headline + body framing it as ByteDance's India edtech business), MediaNama, BusinessToday, Business Standard, Deccan Herald, Pandaily. The Singapore operating entity WhizSolve Pte. Ltd. is explicitly tied to ByteDance by oldrope.club ("Bytedance operates Snapsolve under a Singapore-based entity, WhizSolve Pte. Ltd.") and WhizSolve is confirmed as the app developer on AppAdvice and app-store listings.

Date precision: dateKilled = 2021-11-22 = the Monday on which ByteDance informed the India team (TechCrunch dated Nov 21 says staff told "on Monday"; Nov 22, 2021 was that Monday; Indian outlets carried it Nov 22-23). This is the wind-down decision date. MEDIUM confidence on the exact day.

dateLaunched = 2020-03 is from third-party app trackers (AppBrain/APKPure) reporting the Android package app.homework.solve first appeared on Google Play in March 2020. No official launch press release found. MEDIUM confidence on month.

App lifecycle: last Google Play update Nov 15, 2021 (just before shutdown); rating ~4.35 from ~70k ratings; the Play listing was UNPUBLISHED around Jan 18, 2024 (app-tracker data) — well after operations ceased, so the brand/listing lingered after the team was disbanded.

DISAMBIGUATION (confirmed): the current snapsolve.in / snapsolve.ai sites and Apple App Store "SnapSolve: Homework Solver AI" (id6473076072, by CamelCake / SnapSolve Labs / Abdellatif Abdelfattah), plus Google Play packages com.homeworksolverai.app, com.ekspeace.snapsolve, com.fasttech.snapsolve, are UNRELATED third-party apps reusing the "SnapSolve" name — NOT ByteDance. The genuine ByteDance product is Android package app.homework.solve under WhizSolve Pte. Ltd.

Caveats: no original Apple App Store ID confirmed for the ByteDance version. The "over three dozen / ~36 staff" and "1M+ students" figures come from press reports / app marketing, not audited disclosures.

killedReason: Casualty of China's July 2021 'Double Reduction' (双减) crackdown on for-profit K-12 tutoring, which gutted ByteDance's broader education unit. In the July 2022 Dali Education restructuring, the education business was cut by roughly 80% (down to ~1,000 staff) and at least 50% of the smart-hardware team was laid off; ByteDance decided to stop releasing new lamp models (the hardware business 'had not developed as expected'). The line was effectively wound down — no new models after the Y6 (Dec 2021). By around July 2024 the T6 Pro was delisted from all retail platforms, with official customer service citing a 'company strategic adjustment' and that sales had stopped.
notes:
VERDICT: Genuinely ByteDance (HIGH). Independently confirmed: the Dali Smart brand and dali.com.cn are operated by 北京孔明科技有限公司 (Beijing Kongming Technology), which is 100% owned by ByteDance via wholly-owned subsidiary 北京字节奇点科技有限公司 (NetEase/BJNews coverage of the equity stake). This is a clearer/more accurate operating entity than the draft's 北京大力教育科技有限公司 (which is the Dali Education umbrella); both are retained in developerEntities. The 'kongming' codename in the app bundle id matches this company.

DATE CORRECTION: Y6 launch is Dec 1, 2021 (HIGH — China Daily caijing 2021-12-01, Sina VR 2021-12-01), NOT Dec 7 as the draft suggested. T6/T6 Pro = Aug 5, 2021 (HIGH, more precise than draft's generic 'August 2021'). T5/T5 Pro = Oct 29, 2020, 799 yuan/~$119 (HIGH — TechCrunch, Gizmochina, Pandaily, Bloomberg). 1M cumulative units by Dec 2021 (HIGH — Baidu Baike + Chinese press).

STATUS NUANCE: Kept as 'merged'/wound-down (graveyard-eligible). No single official shutdown announcement, but the death is well-evidenced: (a) July 2022 layoffs + decision to release no new lamp models (last model = Y6, Dec 2021); (b) ~July 2024 T6 Pro delisted from all platforms with customer service citing 'strategic adjustment / stopped sales'; (c) into 2025-2026, widespread T6-series blackscreen/won't-power-on failures with paid-only repair (~400 yuan), users resorting to mtkclient unbricking (Sina Black Cat complaints, CSDN) — i.e. effectively abandoned post-warranty. The brand site dali.com.cn still loads and the 大力家长 app stays listed, so it is an abandoned/discontinued line rather than a fully deleted product. Could equally be tagged 'discontinued'. dateKilled kept as 2022-07 (effective end of the active line); the 2024-07 retail delisting could alternatively be used.

REGION: China-only (HIGH).

name: 悟空搜索 (Wukong Search)
publisher: Beijing Wuxian Weidu Technology Co., Ltd. (北京无限维度科技有限公司 / "Beijing Infinite Dimension Technology"), an indirect wholly-owned ByteDance subsidiary
developerEntities: Beijing Wuxian Weidu Technology Co., Ltd. (北京无限维度科技有限公司, a.k.a. Beijing Infinite Dimension Technology) — indirect wholly-owned ByteDance subsidiary, founded 2021-06-22, registered capital RMB 1M, legal rep Li Fei (李飞)
ByteDance / Douyin Group (字节跳动 / 抖音集团)
appStoreId: 1607244485 (https://apps.apple.com/cn/app/id1607244485 — current listing is 小悟空, an AI toolkit under 抖音集团/Douyin Group; Android package com.wukong.search)
description: ByteDance's ad-free search app, a Baidu challenger. It never dented Baidu and, in the post-ChatGPT rush, was renamed Xiao Wukong in 2023 and made an AI assistant.
killedReason: Not shut down but rebranded/repositioned. As a standalone ad-free search engine, Wukong Search struggled to dent Baidu's dominance and largely re-surfaced ByteDance's own ecosystem content. Amid the post-ChatGPT 2023 generative-AI wave, ByteDance pivoted the app: on 12 Sept 2023 (v1.4) it was renamed 小悟空 (Xiao Wukong) and converted into an LLM-powered AI tool-suite/assistant (on the Yunque/云雀 model), retiring the "Wukong Search" pure-search branding.
notes: RENAME DATE 2023-09-12 (v1.4) is HIGH confidence — corroborated by many contemporaneous Chinese outlets all dated Sept 12, 2023 (Tencent News, ChinaZ, IThome/IT之家, ZOL/中关村在线, MyDrivers/快科技, GamerSky/游民星空, ZhiDx/智东西). The Baidu Baike '2025年5月16日' rename date is an erroneous/late page-edit artifact, contradicted by the overwhelming Sept-2023 reporting; rejected. LAUNCH DATE: kept as 2022-02 (original quiet soft launch, per Baidu Baike and the early-2022 App Store ID 1607244485), but the verifiable press-documented public launch — with the 'ad-free' tagline, days after Tencent retired Sogou's standalone app (Aug 8, 2022) — was AUGUST 2022 (SCMP, Sina explicitly dates Aug 23 2022, TechWeb, Pandaily). MEDIUM confidence on exact launch month. STATUS = rebranded (NOT dead): the app still exists and is actively operating as 小悟空 (Yunque-LLM AI toolkit) under the same App Store ID and com.wukong.search package; only the search-engine branding was retired. ~740K iOS downloads as of late 2023. ByteDance ties verified: developer 北京无限维度科技有限公司 founded 2021-06-22, RMB 1M capital, legal rep 李飞, indirectly wholly owned by 字节跳动有限公司 (Jiemian, Sina, Tianyancha); ran the whole 'Wukong' family (搜索/浏览器/问答). SCMP independently attributes Wukong to 'ByteDance subsidiary Beijing Infinite Dimension Technology.' Current listing under 抖音集团 (Douyin Group). Draft record is accurate as written.

name: 闪电搜索 (Shandian Search)
publisher: 北京抖音信息服务有限公司 (Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd.)
developerEntities: 北京抖音信息服务有限公司 (Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd., formerly Beijing ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd. / 北京字节跳动科技有限公司)
killedReason: Failed to gain traction in a search market dominated by Baidu, and was another in a long line of unsuccessful ByteDance standalone search attempts (after Toutiao Search and Wukong Search). Per Sina Finance (Sept 2024), the app's last update stayed at December 2023 and it was progressively delisted from Chinese app stores thereafter. ByteDance subsequently consolidated its search ambitions around Douyin Search (a standalone search app that rolled out on Huawei/vivo app stores in late August / early September 2024), abandoning the Toutiao-content-based Shandian Search line.
description: A short-lived ByteDance search app built on Toutiao's content. It lasted barely three months before stalling at its 2023 update and vanishing from China's app stores.
notes:
CONFIRMED ByteDance product and CONFIRMED dead. Launched ~late September 2023; 36Kr (2446590743287681) and Tencent/QQ News both dated 25 Sept 2023 report it as "近日" (just) launched. The retrospective Sina Finance article (2024-09-04) explicitly states: "闪电搜索上线于2023年9月，最后一次更新停留在2023年12月。目前，闪电搜索已在各大应用商城逐个下架" (launched Sept 2023, last update Dec 2023, now delisted from major app stores one by one) — this is the basis for dateKilled = 2023-12. Confidence HIGH: status and dates corroborated by reputable tech press (36Kr, Tencent News, The Paper/澎湃, Sina Finance, Woshipm).

DATE PRECISION: Exact launch day not found (sources only give 'late September 2023'); dateLaunched = 2023-09. dateKilled = 2023-12 is the documented last-update date; full delisting was gradual through 2024, so the precise final-removal day is undocumented, but 'dead' is unambiguously correct.

PUBLISHER: 3elife names the publisher as 北京抖音信息服务有限公司 (即原北京字节跳动科技有限公司). The App Store developer-account name could not be independently confirmed because the app is delisted; medium confidence on the exact shell entity, but ByteDance ownership is certain.

APP STORE ID: Not findable — delisted from Chinese app stores, no live listing or numeric ID.

NAME-CLASH WARNINGS (excluded): (1) a third-party desktop file-search utility '闪电搜索' on download aggregators (mydown, downkuai, 32r, bear20) — unrelated; (2) GitHub cybersecurity asset-mapping tools testzboy/FlashSearch and xzajyjs/ThunderSearch — unrelated; (3) a video-streaming/download app '闪电搜索V1.2.3' on OnePlus forum — unrelated.

UNVERIFIED LEAD: one low-quality download-aggregator page (2265.com) claimed the ByteDance app was '更名爱看' (renamed Aikan/'爱看'). This is NOT corroborated by any tech press and aggregator relabeling is unreliable, so it was not adopted; flagging in case future research surfaces a credible rename.

name: 头条搜索 (Toutiao Search)
publisher: Beijing ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd. (北京字节跳动科技有限公司); the rebranded 有柿 app is published under Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd. (北京抖音信息服务有限公司)
developerEntities: Beijing ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd. (北京字节跳动科技有限公司)
Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd. (北京抖音信息服务有限公司)
description: ByteDance's web search app, built to challenge Baidu. It never displaced the leader and in 2023 became Youshi, a lifestyle community; web search lives on in Toutiao.
killedReason: The standalone search app failed to meaningfully erode Baidu's lead amid an overall decline of standalone search use in China (users increasingly searching inside content apps like Douyin/Toutiao). Rather than shut it down, ByteDance repurposed the app: in April 2023 it converted the Toutiao Search app into "有柿" (Youshi), a Xiaohongshu-style image-and-text lifestyle "种草" (content-seeding) community, leveraging Toutiao's existing content library and creators and aligning with Douyin's e-commerce strategy. General web search persisted as an in-app feature of Jinri Toutiao. ByteDance made further separate search attempts (e.g. Wukong Search/悟空搜索, Douyin Search).
notes: Status correctly classified as "rebranded" (not a clean shutdown): the Toutiao Search standalone app was upgraded/renamed into 有柿 (Yǒushì / "Youshi"), a Xiaohongshu-style lifestyle community, in April 2023; web search itself persists as an in-app function within Jinri Toutiao. Two distinct launch milestones, both high confidence: (1) 2019-08-11 = search portal rolled out within Toutiao's website (TechCrunch); internal tests from ~March 2019 (TechNode). (2) Standalone app: TechNode dates release to Feb 20, 2020 (Android store data); Chinese press (Sina Tech, Chinaz, Jiemian) dates the public announcement / app-store rollout to Feb 28, 2020. dateLaunched uses the 2019-08-11 origin. dateKilled = 2023-04 reflects the rebrand to 有柿 (a Tencent App Store listing showed the app-store update dated 2023-04-18; TechNode/Sina/36Kr reported it 2023-04-25), not a true death; medium confidence on the precise day. Could not load Baidu Baike (HTTP 403) to cross-check the canonical Chinese definition, but all other sources align. Do not confuse with separate later ByteDance search efforts: Wukong Search (悟空搜索), Douyin Search (抖音搜索). Continuity proven by Android package name com.ss.android.article.search (ByteDance's com.ss.android namespace), carried over from Toutiao Search to 有柿.

developerEntities: Hudong Online / Beijing Hudong Baike Network Technology Co., Ltd. (北京互动百科网络技术股份有限公司) — pre-acquisition independent operator founded by Pan Haidong (潘海东), retained as the operating entity after the 2019 ByteDance buyout
Beijing Quantum Leap Technology Co., Ltd. (北京量子跃动科技有限公司) — wholly-owned ByteDance subsidiary that took the initial 22.22% stake on 2019-08-14
Beijing Hudong Zhiji Technology Co., Ltd. (北京互动智己科技有限公司) — ByteDance-controlled entity holding the remaining 77.78% to complete 100% ownership
ByteDance (字节跳动) — full owner from September 2019; service surfaced under Toutiao, Douyin and Toutiao Search apps
Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd. (北京抖音信息服务有限公司) — ByteDance app-publishing entity associated with the Douyin Baike rebrand
killedReason: Not a hard shutdown — the Hudong Baike (互动百科) consumer brand was retired through a series of ByteDance rebrands as the company built out its search business to challenge Baidu. ByteDance took a 22.22% stake (RMB 8.1M, via subsidiary Beijing Quantum Leap Technology) on 2019-08-14 and reached 100% ownership by 2019-09-09 (founder team exited). The service was relaunched as a Toutiao Baike (头条百科) test version on 2020-04-30 to serve as the encyclopedia arm of Toutiao Search. The PC brand became Kuaidong Baike (快懂百科) on 2021-03-09; from May 2023 Toutiao Baike was integrated into Douyin and surfaced as Douyin Baike; on 2024-01-04 Toutiao Baike was merged with Shidian Baike (识典百科) and unified under the name Douyin Baike (抖音百科); on 2024-09-26 the PC brand reverted to Kuaidong Baike (快懂百科). The Hudong Baike consumer brand thus ceased to exist, though the underlying entity and content live on as Douyin Baike (mobile) / Kuaidong Baike (PC).
description: Chinese user-editable encyclopedia, founded 2005 as a Baidu Baike rival. ByteDance bought it in 2019 and rebranded it Douyin Baike.
notes: Status is "rebranded," not dead: the Hudong Baike consumer brand was retired but the service survives as Douyin Baike (抖音百科) on mobile / Kuaidong Baike (快懂百科) on PC. For a "Killed by ByteDance" graveyard, the dead entity is the Hudong Baike / Toutiao Baike brand. Founder is Pan Haidong (潘海东 Pān Hǎidōng); one EN-Wikipedia fetch rendered this as "Peng Haidong" — that is a transcription error, Pan/潘 is correct. The Douyin Baike→Kuaidong Baike PC name has flipped back and forth (快懂百科 2021-03-09, then reverted to 快懂百科 2024-09-26 after the Douyin Baike consolidation). Acquisition split: 22.22% via Beijing Quantum Leap Technology (北京量子跃动) + 77.78% via Beijing Hudong Zhiji (北京互动智己), both ByteDance subsidiaries. In May 2025 a Beijing court found the platform liable for unfair competition for scraping ~600,000 entries from Baidu Baike (with fabricated user-edit data to disguise copying), ordering ~RMB 8M (RMB 5M damages + RMB 3M expenses); the ZH-Wikipedia auto-summary mis-rendered this as ¥800M — units error, correct figure is RMB 8M.
successor: Toutiao Baike (later Kuaidong Baike, then Douyin Baike)

幺零贰四（海南）互联网医院有限公司 / 1024 (Hainan) Internet Hospital Co., Ltd. — internet-hospital operator acquired by ByteDance (late 2020), later renamed 海南小荷健康科技有限公司
ByteDance Ltd. (字节跳动) — ultimate parent; '极光/Aurora' health department reports to ByteDance CEO Liang Rubo (梁汝波)

appStoreId: Developer page (Hainan Xiaohe Health Network Technology Co., Ltd): https://apps.apple.com/cn/developer/hainan-xiaohe-health-network-technology-co-ltd/id1633399453 — as of the current listing this developer page shows 小荷AI医生 and 小荷医生 (doctor app, https://apps.apple.com/cn/app/id1535585715) but NOT the consumer 小荷健康 app, which now persists primarily on Android (Tencent 应用宝 com.lvsongguo, v6.4.2, last updated 2026-03-15). Could not confirm a separate stable numeric iOS App Store id for the consumer 小荷健康 app.
description: ByteDance's online doctor-consult and health app. Never shut, but in 2023 its super-app plan faded and the team moved to Douyin's medical arm.
killedReason: Not killed/shut down — significantly downscoped and absorbed into Douyin's medical ecosystem rather than discontinued. After two-plus years of weak traction (none of online consultation, offline clinics, or drug e-commerce had "run through"/become profitable; DAU stayed very low despite Douyin traffic), ByteDance in early 2023 pivoted its health strategy toward Douyin: the 极光/Aurora team was redeployed to operate Douyin's medical vertical (head 吴海锋 still reporting to Liang Rubo), OTC then prescription drug sales moved to Douyin live-commerce, and consultations were routed through a "Xiaohe Health" Douyin mini-program. On 2023-04-28 all official Xiaohe Health patient-communication groups were terminated. Feb 2023 press reports of a full "shutdown" were publicly denied by ByteDance, which stated "小荷健康App并未关停、还在运营中" (the app has not been shut down, still operating) and that the Xiaohe team had not been merged into but was operating Douyin's medical vertical. The standalone consumer app continued; the brand later launched the new standalone "Xiaohe AI Doctor" (小荷AI医生) app on 2025-07-09.
notes: STATUS IS ALIVE, NOT DEAD — do not list a dateKilled. Borderline graveyard candidate: the brand and consumer app are genuinely still operating, and ByteDance even launched a new standalone app (小荷AI医生) on 2025-07-09. If included on a "Killed by ByteDance" page, frame it as "alive / downscoped & absorbed into Douyin" — the standalone health super-app strategy effectively died and folded into Douyin in early 2023. The only dated, defensible "death" of a sub-feature is the 2023-04-28 termination of all patient-communication groups; no valid full app-shutdown date exists, and ByteDance explicitly denied the Feb 2023 "shutdown" reports. Launch is 2020-10 (consumer 小荷健康 app); the 小荷医疗 brand and the 小荷/小荷医生 apps were formally unveiled 2020-11-02; predecessor 绿松果/Green Pine Cone launched 2019-11-07 and was folded into the 小荷 brand in Sept 2020. Region: Mainland China only. Consumer 小荷健康 iOS app is no longer on the current Apple developer page (shows 小荷AI医生 + 小荷医生) but persists on Android (应用宝 com.lvsongguo, v6.4.2, last updated 2026-03-15). Medium confidence only on the exact day-level original launch date and on whether a distinct numeric iOS App Store id for the consumer app still exists.

name: 头号保 (Toutiao Insurance)
category: fintech / insurance brokerage (online insurance marketplace embedded in Douyin & Jinri Toutiao)
publisher: 字节跳动 (ByteDance) — operated via its wholly-owned licensed insurance brokerage 北京华夏保险经纪有限公司 (Beijing Huaxia Insurance Brokers Co., Ltd.), held through 福建字节跳动科技有限公司 (Fujian ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd.)
developerEntities: ByteDance (字节跳动)
Beijing Huaxia Insurance Brokers Co., Ltd. (北京华夏保险经纪有限公司) — the licensed brokerage; ByteDance took 100% control June 2018, new legal rep/exec director approved Nov 2018
Fujian ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd. (福建字节跳动科技有限公司) — 100% parent of Huaxia Brokers
description: ByteDance's online insurance marketplace in Douyin and Toutiao. The 头号保 brand faded around 2021; ByteDance kept the broker license.
killedReason: Low traction in a crowded internet-insurance market — 头号保 "did not stand out from other insurance products" (per jiemian/sina) and became a dormant license-holding business rather than a growth line. ByteDance's broader fintech retreat amid Beijing's 2020–2021 Big Tech finance crackdown deprioritized it further; the 头号保 own brand and its flagship term-life product faded (no longer prominently marketed) around 2021. IMPORTANT: ByteDance did NOT exit insurance — it retained the Huaxia brokerage license and continued brokering insurance (notably inside Douyin). Beijing Huaxia Insurance Brokers reportedly paused new-business intake in February 2025 for a website upgrade while protecting existing policyholders, indicating the underlying brokerage remained operational well after the 头号保 brand faded. There is no clean public shutdown announcement for the 头号保 brand.
notes: Confidence MEDIUM. (1) NAME: canonical brand is 头号保 (Tóuhàobǎo, no 险), read tóuhào ("No.1/top"). Distinct from but successor to the earlier 头条保 / 今日头条保险服务平台 channel — list those as predecessor aliases. (2) LAUNCH: 头号保 brand = Sept 2019 (term-life flagship; multiple Chinese sources agree). Predecessor Toutiao insurance channel: ~Dec 2018 → pulled Jan 7 2019 → relaunched July 18 2019 (Asia Times, TechNode, thepaper). Use 2018-12 for "ByteDance entered consumer insurance," 2019-09 for the 头号保 brand. (3) HUAXIA: ByteDance took 100% of Beijing Huaxia Insurance Brokers (保险经纪), share transfer/control June 25 2018, legal rep approved Nov 5 2018. This is an insurance BROKERAGE — do NOT conflate with the unrelated insurer 华夏人寿 (Huaxia Life), nor with the look-alike 华夏在线保险代理服务有限公司 (huaxonline.com, a Ruizhong Insurance subsidiary, NOT ByteDance). (4) STATUS — "merged" (not "dead"): no source gives a clean shutdown date for 头号保; jiemian (Mar 2022) describes it as underperforming/deprioritized, not formally killed. ByteDance kept the brokerage and continued brokering insurance (inside Douyin); Huaxia Brokers only paused new business Feb 2025 for a site upgrade. The 头号保 brand faded/folded into Douyin's insurance channel rather than cleanly dying. If the site insists on a graveyard binary, "dead" is defensible ONLY for the 头号保 brand+flagship product, with a clear note that the parent brokerage lives on. (5) DATE KILLED ~2021 is INFERRED, not sourced — low confidence; no shutdown announcement exists. (6) APP STORE: no standalone 头号保 app (in-app channel only) → appStoreId empty. (7) The SCMP source covers ByteDance's broad finance downsizing (focus on securities); strongest product-specific sources are the Chinese ones (jiemian, sina, 21jingji, thepaper, zhihu).

name: 晶核 (Crystal of Atlan)

publisher: China: 北京朝夕光年信息技术有限公司 (Nuverse / Beijing Chaoxi Guangnian, ByteDance subsidiary). Global (App Store / Google Play / PlayStation): Nuverse Pte. Ltd. US App Store & Steam: Skystone Games Pte. Ltd. (from June 2025; NOT a ByteDance or Tencent entity).

appStoreId: Global: https://apps.apple.com/app/crystal-of-atlan/id6453111576 (Nuverse Pte. Ltd.); US: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/crystal-of-atlan/id6742167253 (Skystone Games Pte. Ltd.)
killedReason: NOT killed — the game is alive and actively updated (level 70 expansion / "Aerial Village" chapter Jan 15, 2026). The "killed/acquired-shut" premise is FALSE and conflated with other ByteDance projects. ByteDance explored selling Crystal of Atlan (and Earth: Revival) to Tencent in Nov 2023–Jan 2024 during its gaming retreat, but the sale did NOT close; ByteDance confirmed in June 2024 it retained the title (plus Earth: Revival and Moonton) and named Zhang Yunfan Nuverse CEO under a "second phase" gaming strategy. The completed Tencent-backed deal was for the separate C4Games (to China Ruyi Holdings, ~259M yuan). For the 2025 global launch, US publishing shifted from Nuverse to Skystone Games (effective ~June 15, 2025) — a non-ByteDance, non-Tencent firm — mirroring the Marvel Snap arrangement to reduce US regulatory exposure. Nuverse / 朝夕光年 remains developer and operator.

notes: DO NOT list as a "Killed by ByteDance" entry — the game is ALIVE. If included on the site, frame it as "ByteDance's flagship homegrown game that SURVIVED the 2023-24 gaming exit" (US publishing divested to Skystone, but the title itself was NOT sold or shut down). Corrected draft errors: (1) Steam was NOT part of the May 28, 2025 global launch — Steam launched Oct 30, 2025; the May 28 PC launch used a standalone client. (2) The Tencent sale of Crystal of Atlan was PROPOSED but did NOT complete; ByteDance retained the game (confirmed June 2024). VI-GAMES confirmed as the internal dev studio via Chinese Wikipedia.

killedReason: NOT KILLED — the game continues to operate. What happened: in Nov/Dec 2023 ByteDance announced it was restructuring and largely exiting its gaming business (Nuverse), cutting ~1,000 gaming jobs and citing a refocus on long-term strategic growth areas (TikTok/Douyin, e-commerce, music, AI) amid Chinese regulatory pressure and a small (~$1B/2023) gaming revenue base. Unreleased projects were cancelled, but already-launched titles with active players — including Earth: Revival and Crystal of Atlan — were earmarked for divestment, not shutdown. Tencent's purchase of the development studios behind both games was announced ~March 15, 2024. The game has remained live under new ownership: China "classic server" updates ran through at least April 29, 2026 (v1.3.45), and global servers continued weekly maintenance/updates through 2025-2026.
description: Sci-fi survival RPG set on a ruined future Earth, and Nuverse's flagship. When ByteDance quit gaming it sold the studio to Tencent; the game runs on.
notes: CONFIDENCE: HIGH on core facts. The product is genuinely ByteDance-origin (developed by Jiangnan Studio under Nuverse/朝夕光年, ByteDance's gaming arm) — well sourced and not a look-alike. CRITICAL CLASSIFICATION POINT: this is NOT a dead game. The development STUDIO was divested to Tencent (announced ~March 15, 2024), but the GAME continues to operate — China classic server had an update on April 29, 2026 (v1.3.45) and global servers ran weekly updates into 2025-2026 (a Digimon collab was announced for early 2026). For a "Killed by ByteDance" graveyard, this fits only as "left ByteDance / divested" (status=acquired), NOT as a shutdown. If the site tracks only fully-dead products, Earth: Revival arguably should be excluded or clearly flagged as "still live under Tencent." DATES: April 20, 2023 = initial global (JP-led) launch [dateLaunched]; Nov 16, 2023 = China mainland open beta; Aug 15, 2024 = broader English/global launch. description: Chinese-style otome game from ByteDance's Nuverse, mixing cat-raising with five suitors. After losses Nuverse passed it to the developer; it closed on 2023-12-31.
killedReason: Persistent financial losses and staffing collapse. Nuverse announced on July 29, 2022 it would cancel events and cease online operations within the year, citing departures of key team members, insufficient content output, and sustained operating losses. Nuverse's operation officially ended at noon (12:00) on October 31, 2022, with the operating entity transferred to developer Youmeng Chuxin (游梦初心), including official accounts/communities and compliant data migration. The developer relaunched the game on January 5, 2023, but the studio's team disbanded around January 10, 2023 due to force majeure (the company's main entity went into liquidation/bankruptcy), leaving a makeshift team running it. description: Chinese smartphone line, born as Luo Yonghao's Smartisan. ByteDance hired the team in 2019 and shipped Douyin phones, then killed it in January 2021 after weak sales.
killedReason: Commercial failure / strategic pivot. Both ByteDance-era phones sold poorly (the Nut R2 reportedly under 100,000 units, cleared at steep discounts around the shutdown). ByteDance lacked the supply-chain scale of Oppo/Vivo/Xiaomi, so production costs were high, and smartphone hardware was a poor fit for its content/ads-driven business; using a phone to push Douyin did not justify the costs. ByteDance suspended the phone line and redirected the team to its higher-priority education-hardware (Dali smart lamp) unit — which was itself largely curtailed in the 2021 Chinese ed-tech crackdown.
notes: Confidence HIGH that ByteDance acqui-hired the Smartisan hardware team (Jan 2019), shipped the Nut Pro 3 and Nut R2, and suspended the phone business in January 2021. Kill date 2021-01-13 is the internal suspension date confirmed by Chinese press (Tencent News, CBNData, ipeconomy.cn) and English coverage (PingWest, Gizmochina); major English outlets (SCMP, Yicai, The Star) published Jan 16-19, 2021, hence some sources cite ~Jan 19. dateLaunched 2019-11-01 = first ByteDance-era phone (Nut Pro 3 availability). The broader Smartisan brand predates ByteDance (Smartisan T1, 2014; company founded 2012 by Luo Yonghao), but ByteDance only entered in Jan 2019; this record covers the ByteDance era. Nut R2 has two valid launch dates: unveiled Oct 20, 2020; on sale November 2020. No App Store ID — Android phones running Smartisan OS sold in China, not an iOS app. Sub-100k R2 sales figure is from press (Gizmochina/Yicai/SCMP); ByteDance never officially disclosed sales. Status best described as 'acquired-shut'; also legitimately 'dead'.

killedReason: Cancelled in internal development after Pico 4 (launched late 2022) sales fell far short of ByteDance's expectations. ByteDance decided not to compete head-to-head with Meta Quest in consumer VR and instead pivoted: redirecting effort toward a refreshed Pico 4 in the near term and a longer-term high-end Apple Vision Pro competitor codenamed "Swan" (described as still largely experimental/conceptual with no clear timeline). The cancellation followed a large-scale Pico restructuring in late 2023 (roughly 400 staff laid off and ~600 transferred, cutting Pico's headcount by nearly 60%, from ~1,800 to ~800). Pico also reportedly canceled an in-development Beat Saber competitor and handed off Ubisoft's Just Dance VR to another partner.
description: ByteDance's planned next VR headset, to follow the Pico 4. After weak Pico 4 sales it quit the Meta Quest fight and scrapped the Pico 5 in 2023; it never shipped.
notes: CONFIDENCE: medium-high overall on the cancellation; the underlying decision basis is a single primary report rather than an official ByteDance statement. The cancellation is corroborated across multiple reputable VR outlets (UploadVR, Road to VR, MIXED, Pandaily, Gigazine, PhoneArena, SPEEDA Edge), all tracing to a paywalled report by Juro Osawa in The Information published Dec 13, 2023. Because the original is a single sourced report (citing people with direct knowledge), the underlying facts are "reportedly" true. DATE FIELDS: dateLaunched is intentionally empty - the Pico 5 was NEVER launched/released. dateKilled = 2023-12-13, the date the cancellation was first reported (no official ByteDance cancellation date exists; the actual internal decision predates the report). Planned launch was reportedly end of 2024 (per MIXED). APP STORE: no App Store/Google Play ID exists - this is unreleased hardware, not an app. The name "PICO 5" is the reported product name; ByteDance never officially confirmed it. NOTE on restructuring date: kept as "late 2023" because sources do not pin an exact date.

Pico Interactive, Inc. (former US/San Francisco arm, pre-ByteDance)
小鸟看看 Xiaoniao Kankan / Pico Technology Co., Ltd. (original China brand entity, registered Apr 27 2015)
ByteDance Ltd. (parent since Aug 2021)
description: VR headset maker founded 2015, bought by ByteDance in 2021 to fight Meta's Quest. Cut back in 2023 but still going, with the PICO 4 Ultra and 2026 Project Swan.
appStoreId: App "PICO - XR", Apple App Store id 1621284230 (https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/pico-xr/id1621284230); Google Play developer "Pico Immersive Pte. Ltd." (app PICO)
notes: CONFIDENCE HIGH that Pico is ALIVE (scaled back after Nov 2023, not dead). Confirmed by PICO 4 Ultra (Sept 2024) and the actively-marketed 2026 flagship "Project Swan" + Pico OS 6 (officially teased March 2026, US consumer launch planned). ByteDance explicitly denied shutting Pico down. Genuinely a ByteDance product (subsidiary since the 2021 acquisition). UNCERTAINTIES: (1) Acquisition price varies by source: ~RMB 9B/~US$1.39B per Pico's memo vs ~RMB 5B/~US$775M in pre-confirmation rumors — both ranges are sourced. (2) Founding date — legal entity Pico Technology Co., Ltd. registered Apr 27, 2015 (Baidu Baike); corrected draft's "2015-03" to 2015-04. (3) Project Swan launch window reported variously as H1/Q2 2026 to late 2026; treat as 2026, dates not yet final. (4) Project Swan = NEW high-end device; it is NOT the cancelled Pico 5. Pico 5 was a SEPARATE product cancelled in late 2023, after which ByteDance shifted to the longer-horizon Swan project — the draft's note line saying the Nov-2023 restructuring 'cancelled Project Swan, the development project for Pico 5' is WRONG and was corrected here.

name: Dmonstudio
description: Global women's fast-fashion site, widely tied to ByteDance and built to fight SHEIN. It closed abruptly about three months after launch, having won few users.
killedReason: Failed to gain meaningful traction against SHEIN. Reported metrics conflict (one source claimed "over one million users," but more granular figures cited only ~8,000 daily visitors and ~16,400 daily page views in its final days), and its products were priced higher than SHEIN's. ByteDance gave no official explanation for the abrupt closure (and never officially acknowledged ownership); the consensus in Chinese and Western tech press is that low user adoption and an inability to differentiate from SHEIN doomed the project after roughly three months. It was an early, unsuccessful step in ByteDance's broader overseas e-commerce push (later pursued via Fanno, If Yooou, and TikTok Shop).
publisher: Operated under cover/shell entities Guangzhou Chuangxing Media Technology Co., Ltd. (广州创星传媒科技有限公司; listed on the site's "Contact Us" page; established 2021-02-26) and Guangzhou Fanchen Zhishang Technology Co., Ltd. (广州凡尘之上科技有限公司; listed on the site's "Terms of Service" page; established 2021-11-02); widely attributed to ByteDance by Chinese and Western media but never officially confirmed by ByteDance, and no public equity link between these companies and ByteDance was documented
developerEntities: ByteDance (字节跳动) — reported/widely-attributed but undisclosed owner; never officially confirmed
Guangzhou Chuangxing Media Technology Co., Ltd. (广州创星传媒科技有限公司)
Guangzhou Fanchen Zhishang Technology Co., Ltd. (广州凡尘之上科技有限公司)
region: Global (China-operated, overseas-facing; shipped to 100+ countries incl. North America, Europe, Middle East; reportedly blocked U.S. access)
notes: CORRECTED a factual error in the draft: the operating shell entity is Guangzhou CHUANGXING Media Technology Co., Ltd. (广州创星传媒科技有限公司), NOT "Guangzhou C.X Media Technology Co., Ltd. (广州市C.X传媒科技有限公司)" as the draft stated — "C.X" was an erroneous transliteration of 创星 (Chuàngxīng). The second entity is 广州凡尘之上科技有限公司 (Fánchén Zhīshàng = "Above the Mortal World"); draft's pinyin "Fanchen Zhishang" was correct. Per Ebrun, the two companies were established 2021-02-26 (Chuangxing, legal rep Zhang Xu) and 2021-11-02 (Fanchen Zhishang, legal rep Li Yujie), with no documented link between them or to ByteDance. ByteDance ownership is REPORTED/attributed only — never officially confirmed; ByteDance declined to comment. isByteDance treated as true because attribution is consistent and strong across reputable Chinese (Jiemian, 36Kr, Sina, The Paper, Ebrun) and Western (SCMP, BoF, WWD/Sourcing Journal, TechNode) outlets, and it was reportedly an internal S-level ByteDance project under Kang Zeyu — but this is a 'reported, never officially confirmed' case, flagged accordingly. Dates verified: domain registered 2021-11-03 (GoDaddy); shut down 2022-02-11 (~101 days). dateLaunched kept as 2021-11 (registration/internal launch); exact public go-live day undocumented. Product type: primarily a WEBSITE (WWD/Sourcing Journal explicit), though SCMP loosely says "app"; no App Store/Play listing, appStoreId left empty. Conflicting reach figures noted (one million users vs ~8,000 daily visitors). Ex-SHEIN staffing: reports range from "nearly 100" to roughly half of a ~400-person team. Successors: Fanno (late 2021), If Yooou (2022), TikTok Shop.

name: Fanno
description: ByteDance's shopping app selling cheap factory-direct goods to Europe. Closed in 2023 over weak repeat sales, poor quality and slow shipping.
killedReason: Wound down in spring 2022 (team disbanded ~April 2022) and formally ceased operations June 10, 2023 as ByteDance refocused cross-border e-commerce on TikTok Shop. Failure causes: low repurchase/retention (same cheap goods were on Amazon and AliExpress), poor product quality and high returns, slow multi-week logistics and refused refunds, an unsustainable subsidy-driven growth model with seller misconduct once subsidies were cut, uneven traction across markets, failure to convert Facebook-acquired traffic into loyal users, and supply-chain headwinds tied to the Russia-Ukraine war.
region: Europe (Germany, United Kingdom, France, Italy, Spain); China-operated, overseas-facing
publisher: Bytedance Pte. Ltd
developerEntities: Bytedance Pte. Ltd (Singapore shell; iOS developer id 1497663561, the same shell used for many ByteDance overseas apps)
ByteDance Ltd. (parent)
appStoreId: 1591564003 — https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/fanno/id1591564003 (listing now delisted/unavailable)
notes: VERIFIED ByteDance product — confirmed by SCMP, PingWest, TMTPost, Pandaily and others, all explicitly attributing Fanno to ByteDance. LAUNCH DATE: iOS App Store launch is well-sourced as Nov 16, 2021 across multiple independent sources; the broad European marketing/free-shipping push came days before Black Friday (Nov 26, 2021), and SCMP loosely says "last month"/"end of 2021," but Nov 16, 2021 is the precise verified iOS launch date (high confidence). DEATH DATE: two valid milestones — (a) de facto wind-down spring 2022 (team disbanded ~April 2022; last app update ~April 2022), and (b) official website cessation statement "Fanno has officially ceased operations as of June 10, 2023." Used June 10, 2023 as formal dateKilled since it is the only explicit official cessation statement (high confidence on both milestones). Note ByteDance publicly DENIED shutdown rumors in May 2022, so no formal 2022 closure announcement exists. APP STORE ID: 1591564003 is Fanno's app id (matches apps.apple.com URL); 1497663561 is the Bytedance Pte. Ltd developer-page id, NOT Fanno's app id. The "凡诺" Chinese rendering is informal/unverified; Chinese press just called it "Fanno" (overseas-only 出海 product, no widely-used official Chinese brand name). SHEIN-competitor sibling Dmonstudio (also ByteDance, shut Feb 2022) is a separate product, not an alias.

name: 火山小视频 (Huoshan / Douyin Huoshan)
region: China (domestic); separate overseas Vigo Video / Hypstar product line in India, Brazil, the Middle East and other markets
publisher: Beijing Douyin Technology Co., Ltd. (北京抖音科技有限公司) — current App Store developer name; historically associated with Beijing Microlive Vision Technology Co., Ltd. (北京微播视界科技有限公司), the Douyin/Huoshan publishing shell — both ByteDance subsidiaries
developerEntities: ByteDance (字节跳动 / Beijing ByteDance Technology Co., Ltd.)
Beijing Microlive Vision Technology Co., Ltd. (北京微播视界科技有限公司)
Beijing Douyin Technology Co., Ltd. (北京抖音科技有限公司)
appStoreId: 1086047750 (https://apps.apple.com/cn/app/id1086047750) — Android package: com.ss.android.ugc.live
killedReason: ByteDance folded the standalone Huoshan brand into Douyin's ecosystem. On Jan 8, 2020 (announced on a Wednesday; TechNode's English write-up dated Jan 9), ByteDance announced a "brand integration upgrade," renaming 火山小视频 (Huoshan) to 抖音火山版 (Douyin Huoshan Edition), linking the two apps' accounts and sharing content — pulling Huoshan's lower-tier-city user base (~50M DAU at end of 2019) into Douyin to consolidate against Tencent-backed Kuaishou. This ended Huoshan as an independent standalone brand, though the rebranded app continued to operate independently. Separately, the overseas Vigo Video / Vigo Lite line was wound down in 2020 (Brazil and the Middle East first, then India by Oct 31, 2020), with users pushed to TikTok.
description: ByteDance's first short-video app, aimed at China's smaller cities with livestreaming and cash rewards. Folded into Douyin in 2020 and renamed Douyin Huoshan Edition.
notes:
VERIFIED INDEPENDENTLY. ByteDance ownership is solid (high confidence) — Huoshan was ByteDance's first short-video product, built by the Jinri Toutiao team; current App Store developer is the ByteDance subsidiary Beijing Douyin Technology Co., Ltd.

DATE NUANCE — LAUNCH: Best given as 2016-04 (火山直播, livestreaming embedded in Toutiao). The lineage is 火山直播 (Apr 2016) → 火山星球 "Huoshan Planet," 15-sec short-video community (Aug 2016) → renamed 火山小视频 (Dec 2016). Year is high confidence; the consumer-facing 火山小视频 brand specifically dates to Dec 2016.

DATE NUANCE — KILL/MERGE: 2020-01-08 is the announcement date (Caixin, 21财经, zh.Wikipedia, ThePaper, 36Kr); TechNode's English article is dated 2020-01-09. 2020-01-08 used as dateKilled.

STATUS: "merged" is the right single tag. (1) The standalone 火山小视频 BRAND is dead — absorbed into Douyin's account/content ecosystem and renamed 抖音火山版 on 2020-01-08. (2) The China APP is still actively maintained — App Store id 1086047750 shows v39.2.0 last updated within days of research, developer Beijing Douyin Technology Co., Ltd.; so "dead" would be inaccurate for the China app. (3) The OVERSEAS Vigo Video / Vigo Lite line IS fully dead — shut down in Brazil & Middle East first, then India by 2020-10-31, with users pushed to TikTok. If the site wants a clean "killed" entry, Vigo Video can be listed separately as status=dead, dateKilled=2020-10-31.

CORRECTION TO DRAFT'S OVERSEAS FRAMING: The draft treats Vigo Video as simply "the overseas version of Huoshan." More precisely, Vigo Video (earlier Hypstar) descended from Flipagram, a US app ByteDance acquired in Jan 2017, and is a DISTINCT product line — not a straight overseas port of the domestically grown Huoshan (which predates the Flipagram acquisition, having launched as 火山直播 in Apr 2016). I kept Vigo/Hypstar in aliases but relabeled them as a "separate overseas product line." Sources differ on how tightly the two were branded together, so I flagged this rather than asserting a direct port.

PUBLISHER: App Store currently shows Beijing Douyin Technology Co., Ltd.; historically associated with Beijing Microlive Vision Technology Co., Ltd. (the Douyin shell). Android package com.ss.android.ugc.live (the "live" reflects the Huoshan Live origin). The candidate alias "Hot Soup Video" is a mistranslation of 火山 (huoshan = volcano) and was NOT carried forward.

name: Cici
killedReason: Not shut down — rebranded. On November 3, 2025 ByteDance officially renamed the app from "Cici" to "Dola" (announced via the official Dola Facebook channel: "Big news! Cici is now Dola! We've rebranded — same Cici you love, just with a new name"). The same product continued under the new brand: identical App Store numeric ID (6451431247) and Google Play package (com.larus.wolf) retained, existing accounts/chats/settings carried over, and store listings now read "Dola: Formerly Cici." The Cici brand/name is therefore dead, but the underlying app is alive as Dola (10M+ DAU as of Dec 31, 2025, per Pandaily/SAMENA; still live on iOS/Android stores and APKPure v13.7.0 as of June 2026). The rebrand is a strategic consolidation of ByteDance's overseas consumer-AI brand.
region: Global ex-China — region-locked to emerging/overseas markets. Confirmed available: UK, Mexico, Indonesia, Malaysia, Philippines, Brazil (plus reported: Thailand, Spain, France, Poland, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, Argentina). NOT available in mainland China, United States, Canada, or Australia.
publisher: SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD.
developerEntities: SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD. (Spring (SG) Pte. Ltd.) — Singapore subsidiary of ByteDance; App Store developer id 1698257573; same entity used for Coze, ChitChop, Trae and other overseas ByteDance apps
ByteDance Ltd. (ultimate parent / operator; not disclosed on early Cici terms of service or website — ownership first reported by Forbes in Jan 2024)
appStoreId: 6451431247 (https://apps.apple.com/app/id6451431247 — listing now titled "Dola: Smart AI Assistant" / "Dola: Formerly Cici"; was "Cici - Your AI assistant"). Google Play package: com.larus.wolf (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.larus.wolf)
description: ByteDance's overseas AI chatbot, sibling to China's Doubao, on OpenAI and Google models. Renamed Dola in 2025; the app runs on under the new name.
notes:
Independently re-verified from scratch; draft holds up well.

ByteDance ownership: CONFIRMED high. Forbes/OODA Loop (Jan 2024) first reported Spring (SG) Pte. Ltd. as a ByteDance subsidiary running Cici/Coze/ChitChop. Corroborated by Wikipedia (Doubao), The Star (Feb 2026, "The app is called Dola, previously Cici, overseas"), Tracxn legal-entity record, and SCMP (Spring (SG) Pte. Ltd. also publishes ByteDance's Trae code editor). Overseas team led by Alex Zhu (Musical.ly co-founder).

Status = rebranded (NOT shut down) — CONFIRMED. Product alive as "Dola: Smart AI Assistant"; 10M+ DAU as of Dec 31, 2025.

Launch date: CONFIRMED 2023-08 (Wikipedia explicitly: "Dola, the international version of Doubao, was launched in August 2023 as Cici"; China Money Network covered Cici topping charts Nov 2023, impossible if launched Jan 2024). Exact August day not found. The "Jan 2024" figure some sources cite is the Forbes report date, NOT the launch.

Rebrand date: CONFIRMED Nov 3, 2025 via the official Dola Facebook post ("Cici is now Dola"). Set dateKilled = 2025-11-03 to mark the death of the "Cici" brand.

"REMOVED FROM GOOGLE PLAY JAN 23, 2026" CLAIM — judged FALSE / unreliable aggregator noise, NOT recorded as a shutdown. Source is only AppBrain (now 403-blocked) and apkgk, both showing a STALE scrape (last version 11.9.1, last update Jan 20, 2026). Contradicted by: APKPure showing v13.7.0 in June 2026 (a delisted app would not keep shipping newer versions); live Google Play and App Store listings returning in multiple regions (Laos, Antigua) in 2026; Pandaily/SAMENA/The Star coverage showing the app thriving into 2026. No reputable press reported a delisting. A separate plausible-but-US-only signal ("Apple began blocking US users from downloading ByteDance Chinese apps in late Jan 2026") would be a US App Store action, not a global removal — and the US is already in the region-locked-out list, so it does not change status. If a future check shows a genuine global Play delisting, revisit.

Backend: OpenAI GPT (via Microsoft Azure) + Google Gemini for text (confirmed by Wikipedia and Forbes/OODA Loop); ByteDance Seedream/Seedance for image/video (reported, medium confidence). Note this differs from domestic Doubao, which uses ByteDance's own models.

Scope note: Doubao (domestic China app) is a SEPARATE record — Cici/Dola is the ex-China international version. Sibling Spring (SG) apps Coze, ChitChop (and Poligon's BagelBell) are also ByteDance and could be separate records.

Description trimmed to 239 chars (under the 240 limit) for the killedbygoogle.com-style entry; full detail preserved in killedReason and the original long-form description.

Minor cleanups vs draft: link is Wikipedia/Doubao (no dedicated Cici/Dola en.wikipedia page exists). Removed the China-blocked APKMirror/Similarweb/aboutchromebooks aggregator URLs from sources in favor of primary/reputable ones; swapped in The Star and Tracxn. Corrected developerEntities to note BagelBell is Poligon Pte., not Spring (SG).

name: BagelBell
category: AI / entertainment (AI interactive fiction and AI character chat)
region: Overseas / ex-mainland-China markets (Southeast Asia, etc.); not available in the EU; never in mainland China. iOS US/global stores now delisted; Google Play delisted; a geo-restricted web version remained reachable as of mid-2026.
publisher: Poligon Pte. Ltd. (original BagelBell publisher); SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD. (publisher of the rebranded AnyDoor app)
developerEntities: Poligon Pte. Ltd. (Singapore; ByteDance subsidiary that previously published erotic web novels and games for ByteDance) — original BagelBell publisher
SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD. (Singapore; ByteDance subsidiary) — publisher entity for the rebranded AnyDoor app
ByteDance Ltd. / 字节跳动 (ultimate parent; built in-house by ByteDance's 'Flow' department under its Product Development & Engineering org 'PDI', Flow established Nov 2023)
appStoreId: iOS App Store id6473355251 (as 'AnyDoor', https://apps.apple.com/app/anydoor/id6473355251 — iTunes lookup by id and by bundleId com.bagel.anydoor returns resultCount 0 across all checked country stores, app delisted); Android package com.bagel.anydoor (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.bagel.anydoor — HTTP 404 in all checked regions, delisted)
description: ByteDance's overseas AI fiction and character-chat app, launched 2023 on OpenAI's GPT. Renamed AnyDoor in 2024; the mobile apps were later pulled from the stores.
killedReason: Brand retired and folded into a successor app. BagelBell was rebranded to 'AnyDoor: Diverse AI worlds' (same codebase/Android package com.bagel.anydoor, same iOS id6473355251), consistent with ByteDance's 'app factory' / horse-racing strategy of rapidly renaming and consolidating experimental overseas AI apps. The 'BagelBell' name was dropped during 2024 and the publisher entity shifted from Poligon Pte. Ltd. to SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD. The successor AnyDoor mobile apps were later pulled from the Apple App Store (iTunes lookup by id 6473355251 and by bundleId com.bagel.anydoor returns resultCount 0 across all 11 checked country stores) and Google Play (page returns HTTP 404 in all checked regions); only a geo-restricted web version persists. No official shutdown announcement was ever published — ByteDance never publicly acknowledged owning the app.
notes: CONFIDENCE: HIGH overall. (1) ByteDance ownership: HIGH — Forbes (Jan 17, 2024, Emily Baker-White) ties BagelBell to ByteDance via subsidiary Poligon Pte. Ltd.; Pandaily independently confirms it was built by ByteDance's 'Flow' department (PDI org, Flow est. Nov 2023). Multiple independent corroborations (OODA Loop syndication, Market Realist, ChinaMoneyNetwork). Attribution is investigative, not self-disclosed (site/TOS omit ByteDance). (2) Launch: 2023-12 confirmed by Pandaily ('released in December' overseas) + APKPure/APKCombo ('available since December 2023'); Forbes 'past three months' as of Jan 2024 consistent. Exact day not published. (3) Rebrand to AnyDoor: HIGH — APKCombo URL literally reads 'anydoor-formerly-bagelbell'; same Android package com.bagel.anydoor and iOS id6473355251; independent FB community 'Group Anydoor (ex bagelbell) Indonesia'. (4) Publisher: Forbes specifies BagelBell was run by POLIGON (not Spring); the OTHER three apps (Cici/Coze/ChitChop) were run by Spring (SG). The REBRANDED AnyDoor store listings show SPRING (SG) PTE. LTD., so a Poligon-to-SPRING publisher shift occurred during rebrand. Both are ByteDance shells. (5) Status: 'rebranded' is correct. Mobile delisting verified independently 2026-06-15: iTunes lookup id 6473355251 and bundleId com.bagel.anydoor = resultCount 0 in us/sg/gb/id/ph/th/vn/jp/kr/tw/hk; Google Play = HTTP 404 in US/GB/SG/MY/ID. Web version (anydoor.ai / edit.anybagel.com) still live but geo-restricted; last APK update ~Apr 13 2025. No official shutdown announcement. (6) ALIAS CORRECTION: removed the speculative 'Hualu / 话炉' alias — it conflates with the separate domestic app 猫箱/Cat Box (formerly 话炉) and is not a confirmed BagelBell alias. (7) DO NOT CONFUSE with: ByteDance's open-source 'BAGEL' / BAGEL-7B-MoT multimodal model (2025, unrelated); unrelated bagel.ai decision tool and bagel restaurant/food apps. (8) Total downloads ~3.5M (AppBrain). Forbes original URL inferred from the X/Forbes post; OODA Loop (dated 2024/01/17) and Market Realist are confirmed-accessible mirrors of the same Baker-White report. CAVEAT: the surviving geo-restricted web version means the underlying product is not fully dead, but the BagelBell brand and its mobile apps are retired.

Candidate graveyard records worth researching separately: Neihan Duanzi (permanently killed April 2018), Xigua Video (西瓜视频, rebranded Toutiao video channel), TopBuzz (overseas Toutiao-style app, shut down 2020).

description: ByteDance's mid-length video app, once pitched as China's YouTube. Launched 2016 as Toutiao Video, renamed Xigua in 2017; still live, folded into Douyin since 2024.
notes: STATUS = ALIVE, with an important "merged/integrated" caveat worth surfacing. The consumer app is still published and actively updated (China App Store v10.4.0, last updated ~June 2026; v9.x builds across 2025 on Android stores). However, Xigua Video has been progressively absorbed into Douyin's ecosystem and is no longer a fully independent product: (1) Jan 2, 2024 press (Tencent News/QQ, NBD, DoNews) reported "抖西合流" — Xigua's operations team folding into Douyin, with Xigua content distributed inside Douyin and no separate Xigua operating strategy. Note: the qq.com article at 20240102A04RX300 itself relies on anonymous sources and does NOT contain an official denial; the Douyin denial ("相关信息不实，公司会长期持续投入服务好西瓜视频的作者和用户") is reported in separate coverage (Sohu/ennews/zhihu) dated around Jan 2, 2024. (2) In Aug/Sep 2024 the long-running "中视频伙伴计划" (Mid-Video Partner Program, launched 2021 across Toutiao+Xigua+Douyin) was retired and upgraded to a unified Douyin "创作者伙伴计划"; Xigua's independent creator/upload platform was wound down, Xigua accounts became Douyin accounts, and Xigua+Douyin revenue is now settled unified through Douyin (Toutiao settled separately). So the front-end app survives while the account system and monetization are merged into Douyin — defensibly categorizable as "merged/integrated (ongoing)" rather than fully independent, but NOT dead. Dates: launch May 2016 (month/year high confidence; exact day not reliably sourced); rename to Xigua 2017-06-08 (high confidence, multiple sources). App Store seller is currently "Beijing Douyin Information Service Co., Ltd." (confirmed on the live id1134496215 listing and on Xiaomi/Baidu/360 Android stores).

The iOS app was delisted from the App Store (iTunes Search API returns 0 results in the US, and a bundleId lookup for com.wukong.nsai returns 0, June 2026). The chitchop.com homepage went dark (last live Wayback capture 2024-10-08; redirect by 2024-12-08; gone by 2025-03-21) and now 301-redirects to www.chitchop.com, which returns HTTP 404; only legacy legal/terms pages (e.g., /auto-rule/i18n/ns/user/en) still return HTTP 200, so a backend sliver lingers but the consumer product is dead. Context: ByteDance was rapidly launching, testing, and culling multiple low-profile overseas generative-AI apps (the four exposed by Forbes in Jan 2024 were described as still testing) and consolidated its consumer AI effort around Doubao (China) and Cici (overseas, later rebranded Dola in Dec 2025); ChitChop did not survive that consolidation. No relaunch or rebrand of ChitChop was found.

description: Trae: ByteDance's AI code editor, a fork of VS Code with a built-in agent for chat-driven coding. Launched January 2025; still developed and growing.
notes:
VERDICT: Genuinely a ByteDance product and ALIVE/actively growing as of mid-2026 — NOT a graveyard candidate (include only if the site lists living products for context). Description trimmed to <=240 chars per style spec; full detail retained below.

LAUNCH DATE: International edition ~Jan 20, 2025 (SCMP article Jan 21, 2025; some sources cite Jan 19 first availability, Jan 20 general release). China edition Mar 3, 2025 (TechNode + 36Kr + aibase). Windows version Feb 2025.

OWNERSHIP: Confirmed ByteDance. International edition published by Spring (SG) Pte. Ltd. (same Singapore shell behind Cici/Coze). China edition by ByteDance directly.

MODELS: International edition launched free with GPT-4o + Claude 3.5 Sonnet (later Claude 3.7/4, DeepSeek R1, newer); paid Pro ~May 2025. China edition on Doubao/Seed models (Doubao-1.5-Pro, later Doubao-Seed-Code; Doubao-Seed-2.0 Code integrated Feb 14, 2026) plus DeepSeek R1/V3.

SCALE/ROADMAP: ~1.6M MAU and 6M+ registered users by end of 2025. TRAE 2.0 SOLO preview Jul 21, 2025; standalone SOLO (desktop+web, Code and "MTC/More Than Coding" modes) launched Mar 31, 2026; IDE at v3.x mid-2026.

MARSCODE/LINEAGE: Confirmed — ByteDance unified AI dev tools under the Trae brand; MarsCode became "Trae Plugin (formerly MarsCode)," standalone China assistant is "Doubao MarsCode." Medium-high confidence on exact rebrand mechanics.

APP STORE: Flagship is a DESKTOP IDE from trae.ai (no canonical App Store id). Id 6761401019 is the iOS mobile companion "TRAE - AI Work Assistant" / "TRAE - SOLO AI Work Assistant" by SPRING(SG)PTE.LTD. Google Play package: com.bytedance.trae.overseas.

"THE REAL AI ENGINEER": Backronym in third-party coverage, NOT confirmed official — low confidence; kept as labeled community alias.

TELEMETRY: Unit 221B (Mar 2025) + The Register (Jul 28, 2025) flagged extensive telemetry reportedly continuing after opt-out — persistent connections to ByteDance domains, a persistent machineId identifier, and local WebSocket channels.

For a "Killed by ByteDance" graveyard this product should be EXCLUDED from the dead list (status=alive; include only as context if desired). Launch date Jan 19, 2021 is well-sourced (CNBC, American Banker, Gizmochina, Yahoo all dated Jan 19, 2021; Chinese sources state 2021年1月19日). Acquisition-date nuance: English outlets citing "acquired Ulpay in 2018" refer to when the Hezhong Yibao partnership BEGAN (services across Toutiao, Douyin, Huoshan, Pipixia, Duoshan); the actual OWNERSHIP/control transfer was Aug 28, 2020 when Zhang Yiming's Tianjin Tongrong E-Commerce took 100% of shares (Yicai Global Sep 3, 2020; Caixin Sep 4, 2020). License issued by PBOC in 2014; firm founded 2012. The 2024 offline-license target (Union Mobile Pay / 联动优势) and the ~$190M figure are reported by TechNode; exact price varies — MEDIUM confidence on that amount only. Jan 2, 2025 capital increase to RMB 3.15B confirmed by cls.cn and stcn.com. Aug 2025 Apple App Store testing confirmed by SCMP, MacRumors, AppleInsider. Description tightened to <=240 chars per style guide.

name: TikTok Shop
publisher: TikTok Pte. Ltd. (Singapore; the ByteDance entity used on the App Store / Google Play for the Seller Center companion app)
developerEntities: TikTok Pte. Ltd. (Singapore — Google Play & App Store publisher for TikTok Shop Seller Center)
ByteDance Ltd. (ultimate parent)

TikTok USDS Joint Venture LLC (US operations entity post-Jan 2026 deal; Oracle/Silver Lake/MGX-led, ByteDance ~19.9% minority)
appStoreId: 1591003012 — https://apps.apple.com/us/app/tiktok-shop-seller-center/id1591003012 (TikTok Shop Seller Center). Android: com.tiktokshop.seller on Google Play. Consumer side is embedded in the main TikTok app (com.zhiliaoapp.musically).
description: TikTok's in-app shopping marketplace, with a Shop tab, livestream selling and an affiliate program. Still alive; banned in Indonesia in 2023, then merged into Tokopedia.
notes:
STATUS = ALIVE and growing — NOT a dead product. Belongs on a "Killed by ByteDance" site only as a partial/regional entry for scoped shutdown events, clearly marked:

1) Indonesia shutdown then merge/rebrand (HIGH confidence): TikTok Shop's in-app e-commerce was banned in Indonesia effective ~Oct 4, 2023 under Trade Ministry Regulation No. 31/2023 (banning transactions on social media). TikTok announced a deal (Dec 11, 2023) to take a controlling ~75% stake in GoTo's Tokopedia (~$840M for the stake plus a $1.5B+ investment commitment), relaunched shopping under Tokopedia with the "Beli Lokal" campaign on Dec 12, 2023. The merger of Tokopedia and TikTok Shop Indonesia under the PT Tokopedia entity COMPLETED on January 31, 2024. So in Indonesia it was effectively "killed and merged/rebranded," then revived.

2) US App-Store removal (temporary, resolved): The TikTok Shop Seller Center app was pulled from US Apple App Store and Google Play on Jan 19, 2025 alongside TikTok and other ByteDance apps under PAFACA, and restored on Feb 13, 2025 after the Trump non-enforcement order and AG Bondi assurance letter. Press coverage focused mainly on the flagship TikTok app; the Seller Center removal was part of the broader ByteDance-app sweep.

3) 2026 ownership nuance: On Jan 22, 2026 ByteDance closed a deal transferring control of TikTok's US operations to "TikTok USDS Joint Venture LLC" (Oracle/Silver Lake/MGX-led; ByteDance retains ~19.9% minority). Affects only US operations — globally TikTok Shop remains a ByteDance product. isByteDance = TRUE confirmed.

Recommendation: list as partial/regional/temporary entry; overall status ALIVE — not a full tombstone. dateLaunched corrected to 2021-04 (Indonesia full launch); UK late 2021; US formal launch Sept 12, 2023. App Store id1591003012 confirmed; exact Seller Center first-release date not obtained (MEDIUM).

killedReason: Divested, not shut down: ByteDance sold 100% of Tuchong to Jishi Design in October 2024 amid its retreat from text-and-image (图文) businesses; the platform continues under new ownership.

notes: ByteDance's first external acquisition (Dec 2014); 100% equity sold to 即时设计 (Jishi Design) in Oct 2024 — a divestiture, lives on under new owner. Founding year ~2011.

ByteDance ownership confirmed by TechNode, SCMP, PingWest/Yahoo, 36Kr and Baidu Baike (Zhang Yiming was an early shareholder/co-founder before ByteDance Ltd. took control in Aug 2017). Distinct from all known camera/editing apps (Faceu, Xingtu, Qingyan, Hypic, Whee, Pixsoul, Limao Camera) — it is a cloud-storage backup album, not on the known list. Date caveat: multiple sou
descZh: 时光相册（Everphoto）是一款中国的智能云相册应用，提供 AI 人脸识别自动分类、加密云端备份和共享相册功能，并曾凭借一款新海诚《你的名字》风格的照片滤镜走红。字节跳动于 2017 年收购了其开发商上海时光无限科技；该应用于 2023 年 8 月 17 日停止接收上传，并在 2023 年 11 月 15 日前后停止运营，之后已备份的照片视频被清空。
reasonZh: 在字节跳动“降本增效”、重新分配资源的浪潮中被关停；云相册业务既不直接创收，也不支撑核心业务，还面临中国通用云存储服务带来的同质化竞争。
categoryZh: 云端照片存储／AI 智能相册

description: A ByteDance mobile music-creation app first launched on 16 May 2022 as 海绵乐队 (Sponge Band), a recording-studio / vocal-optimization / one-click-Remix / auto-Mashup editing tool serving Soda Music and Douyin users. In June–August 2024 it was renamed and pivoted to 海绵音乐 (Sponge Music), an AI text/lyric-to-song generator (a Suno rival) powered by ByteDance's Doubao music-generation model, where users input a one-line prompt, lyrics, or an image to generate full songs.
killedReason: Not shut down — rebranded and repositioned. The original 海绵乐队 manual music-editing tool gained little traction; once ByteDance's Doubao music-generation model matured, the product was renamed 海绵音乐 in July 2024 (public launch Aug 2, 2024; closed beta from June 5, 2024) and re-focused on generative AI song creation to compete with Suno.
notes: Distinct from Soda Music (汽水音乐, streaming) and from Doubao (豆包, which supplies the underlying AI music model). The 2022 launch was explicitly framed by ByteDance as a creation tool for Soda Music and Douyin users. Candidate's claimed sources and timeline are accurate; only refinement is that the AI rename/pivot occurred July 2024 (beta June 5, public Aug 2, 2024), not generically "2024."

reasonZh: 并未被关停——而是更名并重新定位。原“海绵乐队”手动音乐编辑工具用户增长有限；待字节跳动豆包音乐生成模型成熟后，该产品于 2024 年 7 月更名为“海绵音乐”（6 月 5 日起内测，8 月 2 日公开上线），转向生成式 AI 歌曲创作，与 Suno 竞争。

description: Ripple was a free AI-powered music-creation, composition and audio-editing iOS app from ByteDance, launched as a US invite-only closed beta on June 30, 2023. Its flagship 'Melody to Song' feature let users hum or sing a melody and the AI would generate instrumental accompaniment (drums, bass, guitar, piano) across genres; it also included a 'virtual recording studio' for cutting and rearranging audio, with the AI model trained on music licensed/owned by ByteDance or produced in-house.
killedReason: No formal shutdown was ever announced. The app never appears to have exited its US iOS invite-only closed beta or rolled out more widely; press coverage stops after 2023 and the official ripple.club site returns no content, indicating the beta was quietly wound down/abandoned. ByteDance's overseas AI-music ambitions appear to have shifted to in-house tools and patents rather than this standalone consumer app.
notes: VERDICT: Genuinely ByteDance, genuinely new (not in known list), keep. Ownership is high-confidence (Music Ally, MBW, TechCrunch, Engadget, Billboard all attribute it to ByteDance directly). Distinct from Resso/TikTok Music (streaming), SoundOn (distribution), Soda Music 汽水音乐, and CapCut — it is a creation/composition app. NOTE the same coverage also surfaced a SEPARATE earlier ByteDance music app

description: Jukedeck was a London-based AI music startup (founded 2012 by Ed Newton-Rex, with Patrick Stobbs) whose flagship product "Jukedeck Make" let users automatically generate royalty-free background music using neural-network-based composition, with customizable genre, mood, instruments, length and tempo. ByteDance acquired the company in July 2019; the website and product were taken offline (homepage replaced with a holding message) and the team folded into ByteDance's AI Lab, where founder Ed Newton-Rex became Director.
killedReason: Acqui-hired by ByteDance in July 2019 primarily for its AI music-generation technology and talent (to reduce reliance on costly music licensing for Douyin/TikTok and bolster ByteDance AI Lab). The standalone consumer product/website was shut down and never relaunched; the technology and team were absorbed internally. Founder Ed Newton-Rex later left ByteDance around 2021.
notes: Strongly sourced across English and Chinese press. Distinct from all known ByteDance music products (Resso, TikTok Music, SoundOn, Soda Music) — Jukedeck was AI music GENERATION, not streaming/distribution. Its tech reportedly underpinned later ByteDance AI music efforts. Clients before acquisition included Coca-Cola, Google, UKTV, and the Natural History Museum London; over 1M tracks generated.

reasonZh: 字节跳动于 2019 年 7 月对其进行收购式招募，主要看中其 AI 音乐生成技术和人才（以减少抖音/TikTok 对高成本音乐授权的依赖）。独立消费级产品/网站被关停且再未重启，技术和团队被内部吸收；创始人后于 2021 年前后离开。
categoryZh: AI 音乐生成（免版税音乐创作）

description: 幸福里 (Xingfuli) was ByteDance's real estate listings and transaction platform. It originated from Toutiao's property channel, launched in 2018 as 好房多 (Haoduofang), and was renamed 幸福里 in 2019. It listed new and secondhand homes, aggregated agency inventory (麦田, 我爱我家, etc.), and monetized via portal/lead fees, leveraging Douyin and Toutiao traffic.
killedReason: Weak Chinese property market. A 2021 plan to spin off Xingfuli with external capital (Vanke, Country Garden, Sequoia reportedly courted) never closed. In May 2023 ByteDance exited offline brokerage, handing its 小麦房产 offline operation to 麦田 and repositioning Xingfuli as Douyin's online real estate mid-platform. In May 2024 it was folded into Douyin Life Services (抖音生活服务), ending its independent existence.
notes: Distinct from 懂车帝 Dongchedi (autos) and from 住小帮 (home renovation, which was merged into Douyin Life Services in the same May 2024 restructuring — itself a separate ByteDance product not in the known list, worth checking). "Happy Home" is a literal gloss; conventional romanization is Xingfuli. Verified independently via Sina Finance, Tencent News (QQ), Jiemian, Guancha, SCMP, and Yahoo Finance — a
descZh: 幸福里是字节跳动旗下的房产信息与交易平台。它源自今日头条的房产频道，于 2018 年以“好房多”之名上线，并在 2019 年更名为“幸福里”。它发布新房和二手房信息，聚合中介房源（麦田、我爱我家等），并依托抖音和今日头条的流量，通过门户/导客费用变现。
reasonZh: 中国房地产市场低迷。2021 年引入外部资本分拆的计划未能落地。2023 年 5 月字节跳动退出线下中介，将小麦房产线下业务移交麦田，把幸福里重新定位为抖音线上房产中台；2024 年 5 月它被并入抖音生活服务，结束独立存在。

descZh: 海豚财富是字节跳动“海豚”金融产品线中的一个金融资讯／财富平台——“海豚”这一前缀由创始人张一鸣亲自选定——与模拟交易应用海豚股票并列。它在字节跳动 2021—2022 年退出金融／证券业务期间被关停。
reasonZh: 作为字节跳动 2021—2022 年从金融／证券业务整体撤退的一部分而被关停。同门产品海豚股票及运营主体文星在线于 2022 年 2 月以约 2000 万元出售给华林证券；随着字节跳动表示不再涉足证券业务，整条海豚金融产品线被关停／剥离。

descZh: 松鼠证券是字节跳动旗下的香港在线证券经纪商，约于 2019 年底/2020 年注册，取得香港证监会第 1、4、5、6、9 类牌照，可提供证券和期货交易、投资/期货咨询、机构融资咨询及资产管理服务，面向海外（港股/美股）股票。它与另行搭建的恒星证券以及中国大陆的海豚股票行情应用一起，构成了字节跳动的海外证券版块。
reasonZh: 在跨境互联网券商监管收紧、参与度低迷（据报道约 20 万日活）之际，字节跳动从金融科技布局中撤退。2022 年 2 月宣布彻底剥离证券业务、不再涉足；海豚股票售予华林证券，松鼠证券与恒星证券则被出售或关停。

descZh: 恒星证券是字节跳动于 2021 年 1 月通过收购成立已久的本地券商亚洲太平证券有限公司（Asia Pacific Securities Limited，1996 年成立）、并在 2021 年 3 月 5 日将其更名而来的一家香港券商。它是字节跳动更广泛的金融科技/证券布局的一部分（与海豚股票应用和松鼠证券并列），但始终是一个内部测试主体，从未正式向公开市场开放。
reasonZh: 在科技/金融科技整顿、香港科技股估值下跌及跨境互联网券商在中国大陆“无牌”经营受监管审查之际，字节跳动于 2022 年 2 月宣布全面退出证券业务，将海豚股票及文星在线售予华林证券，并剥离或内部关停其他证券主体，恒星证券最终被关停。

descZh: 抖音月付是字节跳动嵌入抖音App的“花呗”式先享后付消费信贷产品，提供免息期及3/6/12期分期，可用于App内消费及抖音电商购物。它于2020年底以“放心花”之名推出（最初仅用于购买DOU+推广工具），2020年12月/2021年1月重新上线为“DOU分期”，并于2022年7月22日更名为抖音月付；授信资金由字节跳动控股的深圳中融小额贷款提供。

The standalone 备用金 brand appears to have faded as ByteDance consolidated its consumer-lending under the licensed entity Shenzhen Zhongrong Microloan, with the surviving consumer-facing brands being 放心借 (Fangxin Jie) and 抖音月付/DOU分期 (Douyin Monthly Pay). descZh: 备用金是字节跳动于2020年7月24日在今日头条App上推出的小额短期应急现金贷产品。它提供约500至2000元的循环贷款，还款期为7天（首次使用免息，之后日利率约0.056%/年化约20.44%），最初为引流/助贷服务，后转为由字节跳动持牌主体深圳中融小贷承做的自营现金贷。

killedReason: The standalone Manfen app never gained traction (Android-only, iOS never shipped); ByteDance consolidated its consumer-lending business into the in-app 放心借 (Fangxinjie) and 抖音月付 products inside Douyin/Toutiao under separate operating entities, leaving the standalone Manfen app effectively abandoned and unsearchable on Android stores.

NOT in known list and no
descZh: 满分是字节跳动于2019年10月10日低调推出的一款独立安卓消费金融App，提供现金分期贷款（最高约20万元）、消费信贷/分期以及银行联名信用卡。它充当字节跳动/今日头条“放心借”借贷平台的专属App前端——满分本身是助贷/导流平台，将用户对接到持牌银行（如新网银行、华夏银行），而非直接放贷方。
reasonZh: 独立的满分App始终未能打开局面（仅有安卓版，iOS从未上线）；字节跳动将消费信贷业务整合进抖音/今日头条内、由不同运营主体承做的放心借和抖音月付产品，致使独立的满分App实际被弃用，在安卓应用商店也搜索不到。

description: 摸摸鱼 (Momoyu) was ByteDance's casual / instant-play mini-game platform ("game box") launched in April 2021. Users played lightweight casual games and earned a virtual reward token called 小鱼币 (Xiaoyu coins / "little fish coins") by completing tasks (browsing/launching/rating games, joining player groups), which could be redeemed for ad-free passes and physical prizes such as blind boxes and gacha toys; the app hit ~9M MAU within three months of launch and monetized mainly via in-game advertising, with a (later-abandoned) game-streamer/livestream sponsorship program.
killedReason: Shut down March 29, 2024 (less than three years after launch) as part of ByteDance's broad retreat from gaming (layoffs/wind-down at Ohayoo and Nuverse/朝夕光年). Casual-game competition was fierce and monetization difficult; its streamer livestream-sponsorship program had effectively stalled by 2022. On shutdown the 小鱼币 redemption shop closed and unredeemed virtual assets were forfeited with no compensation.
notes: VERDICT: CONFIRMED genuine, new, ByteDance-owned, and dead. Multiple independent reputable outlets (Jiemian, Tencent News, 36Kr, DoNews, Sohu, NetEase, GameLook) explicitly describe it as "字节跳动旗下小游戏平台" (ByteDance's mini-game platform); it ran Douyin-account login and hosted ByteDance Ohayoo casual games. NOT in the ALREADY-KNOWN list and not an alias of any known product. CAUTION on look-alikes: u
descZh: 摸摸鱼是字节跳动于2021年4月推出的休闲/即点即玩小游戏平台（“游戏盒子”）。用户玩轻量休闲游戏，并通过完成任务（浏览/启动/评价游戏、加入玩家群）赚取名为“小鱼币”的虚拟奖励代币，可兑换免广告权益及盲盒、扭蛋玩具等实物奖品；上线三个月内月活跃用户达约900万，主要靠游戏内广告变现，并曾有一个（后被放弃的）游戏主播/直播赞助计划。
reasonZh: 2024年3月29日关停（上线不足三年），系字节跳动全面收缩游戏业务（Ohayoo及朝夕光年裁员/收尾）的一部分。休闲游戏竞争激烈、变现困难；其主播直播赞助计划到2022年已基本停滞。关停时小鱼币兑换商城关闭，未兑换的虚拟资产被作废且无补偿。

It combined game discovery, reservations, downloads, rankings and user reviews, aiming to become a distribution channel for mid-to-hardcore games — a step beyond ByteDance's earlier casual-game platform 摸摸鱼 (Momoyu).
killedReason: Never advanced past closed testing to a full public launch; quietly abandoned as ByteDance retreated from gaming and game distribution, culminating in the November 2023 wind-down of its games arm 朝夕光年 (Nuverse). descZh: 灵选是字节跳动一款类似TapTap/WeGame的手游社区与分发App，2022年1月底在抖音内开启封闭测试，口号为“灵机一动，选到好游戏”。它集游戏发现、预约、下载、排行和用户评测于一体，意在成为中重度游戏的分发渠道——比字节跳动早期的休闲游戏平台摸摸鱼更进一步。
reasonZh: 始终未从封测推进到正式公开上线；随着字节跳动收缩游戏及游戏分发业务，并在2023年11月关停游戏部门朝夕光年，灵选被悄然放弃。它是2022年初一批“App工厂”实验（与派对岛、悟空搜索同期）中未能存活的产品之一。

深圳市脸萌科技有限公司 / Shenzhen Lianmeng Technology Co., Ltd.
description: 可颂 (Kesong, "Croissant"; internal codename Sicily) is ByteDance's Xiaohongshu-style lifestyle/种草 community app, tightly linked to Douyin so creators can port short-video content into an image-and-text seeding feed. It launched 11 Jul 2022 and was pulled from app stores within ~20 days, relaunched 31 Mar 2023, relaunched again Apr 2024 (v2.1.0), and by 2025 was repositioned as a "photography enthusiasts' sharing community" with AI camera features ("机位"/AI composition).
killedReason: Repeatedly pulled/restarted rather than permanently killed: the early versions failed to build a genuine seeding community because Douyin-sourced content was too entertainment-oriented to generate Xiaohongshu-style purchase recommendations. As of June 2026 the app is still being updated (e.g., Tencent Yingyongbao shows v38.5.1 dated 2026-06-10) and is therefore best classified as alive, despite its multiple shutdown/relaunch cycles.
notes: Genuinely ByteDance (operated by ByteDance subsidiary 脸萌科技, the CapCut/轻颜 team; Android package com.ss.android.ugc.sicily uses ByteDance's com.ss.android namespace). NOT a duplicate of any known product: distinct from 新草 Xincao (ByteDance's earlier 2018 Toutiao-based seeding app, already known/dead), distinct from 有柿 Youshi (rebranded Toutiao Search lifestyle community, already known), and distinc
descZh: 可颂（内部代号Sicily）是字节跳动一款类似小红书的生活方式/种草社区App，与抖音紧密打通，创作者可将短视频内容搬运到图文种草信息流中。它于2022年7月11日上线，约20天内即从应用商店下架，2023年3月31日重新上线，2024年4月再次重新上线（v2.1.0），到2025年被重新定位为“摄影爱好者分享社区”，并加入AI相机功能（“机位”/AI构图）。
reasonZh: 属反复下架/重启而非彻底关停：早期版本因抖音来源内容过于娱乐化、难以产生小红书式购买推荐，未能建立起真正的种草社区。截至2026年6月该App仍在更新（如腾讯应用宝显示v38.5.1，日期为2026-06-10），故尽管经历多次关停/重启周期，仍应归类为存活。
categoryZh: 生活方式 / 种草社区（后转型为摄影社区）

description: 小趣星 (Xiaoquxing) was a children's interest/educational short-video app launched by ByteDance's Dali Education unit on ~September 20, 2021, billed as ByteDance's third short-video app after Douyin and Douyin Volcano version. It served age-matched, screen-time-limited (~40 min/day) curated content across 34 categories (humanities, science, picture books, nursery rhymes) sourced from Douyin teen-mode creators, with no recording, posting, commenting, sharing, or social features.
killedReason: Discontinued amid the collapse and restructuring of ByteDance's Dali Education business following China's 2021 "double reduction" (双减) policy on after-school tutoring, which forced ByteDance to wind down or pivot most of its education products after heavy losses and layoffs.
notes: ByteDance ownership confirmed via app-store publisher entity (Beijing Future Smart Learning Education Technology Co., Ltd., a wholly-owned Dali Education subsidiary that also operated GuaGuaLong English) and multiple independent media (Sina Finance, Jiemodui, Sohu, 36Kr-cited reports). Discontinuation confirmed by Chinese Wikipedia's ByteDance article, which lists 小趣星 under the '已停止运营' (ceased ope
descZh: 小趣星是字节跳动大力教育部门于2021年9月20日前后推出的一款儿童兴趣/教育短视频App，号称是继抖音和抖音火山版之后字节跳动的第三款短视频App。它提供按年龄匹配、限制使用时长（约每天40分钟）的精选内容，涵盖人文、科学、绘本、儿歌等34个品类，内容源自抖音青少年模式的创作者，不设录制、发布、评论、分享等社交功能。
reasonZh: 在中国2021年“双减”课外辅导政策导致字节跳动大力教育业务崩塌与重组后停运；该政策迫使字节跳动在巨额亏损和裁员后关停或转型大部分教育产品。
categoryZh: 儿童教育短视频

name: 百万英雄 (Million Hero)
category: Live trivia quiz / interactive game show
publisher: 西瓜视频 (Xigua Video), a ByteDance / Toutiao platform; produced by Xigua Video with 今日头条 (Toutiao) and 抖音 (Douyin) as co-producers
developerEntities: 字节跳动 ByteDance
今日头条 Toutiao
西瓜视频 Xigua Video
抖音 Douyin
description: 百万英雄 (Million Hero) was a free, large-scale live-streamed trivia quiz game show launched by ByteDance's Xigua Video in January 2018, riding the early-2018 China live-quiz craze. A host asked twelve multiple-choice questions; players who answered all correctly within ~10 seconds each split a cash prize pool of RMB 50,000 to 1 million, with a "revival card" earned by sharing/inviting friends across ByteDance's app matrix (a major user-acquisition mechanic). It ran 278 sessions with nearly 300 million cumulative participants before Season 1 ended; it was rebranded/relaunched as 头号英雄 (Toutiao Hero) in December 2019, which is also now defunct.
killedReason: Season 1 ended February 15, 2018 after China's SARFT (广电总局) issued a directive requiring live online quiz programs to hold an "Information Network Audiovisual Program Transmission License" — the same crackdown that shut down 冲顶大会, 百万赢家, and other rivals. ByteDance later relaunched a compliant successor (头号英雄) under Xigua Video's license in Dec 2019, which subsequently wound down as the live-quiz fad faded.
notes: Treated by the candidate as the same product line that later became 头号英雄 — this is accurate; 头号英雄 was the rebranded, license-compliant successor. The product was a flagship example of the 2018 Chinese live-trivia bubble and a ByteDance growth/user-acquisition tactic for its app ecosystem. Distinct from all ~150 already-known entries (not a translation/alias of any of them).
descZh: 百万英雄是字节跳动旗下西瓜视频于2018年1月推出的免费大型直播答题游戏节目，赶上了2018年初中国的直播答题热潮。主持人提出十二道多选题，在每题约10秒内全部答对的玩家瓜分5万至100万元人民币的现金奖池，通过分享/邀请好友可在字节跳动App矩阵中获得“复活卡”（一项重要的拉新机制）。它共举办278场，累计参与者近3亿，随后第一季结束；2019年12月更名/重启为“头号英雄”，该产品如今也已停运。
reasonZh: 第一季于2018年2月15日结束，因中国广电总局发文要求网络直播答题节目须持《信息网络传播视听节目许可证》——同一轮整顿也关停了冲顶大会、百万赢家等竞品。字节跳动后于2019年12月以西瓜视频牌照重启合规继任产品“头号英雄”，但随直播答题热潮退去也逐渐停运。
categoryZh: 直播答题 / 互动游戏节目

name: 幕布 (Mubu)
publisher: Originally Beijing Kundu/founder team; under ByteDance merged into Feishu (飞书); now operated by Shenzhen Shili Hu Technology Co., Ltd. (深圳市十里湖科技有限公司, flomo's operator)
